action.reasoning: Observation: Current predict.py (BFS+utility) gives 0% success in local test; robot explores only ~30% and stops. Analysis: The policy fails because when no node has utility>0 (likely due to undiscovered free cells), it falls back to unvisited nodes but may get stuck. Also visited_set prevents revisiting necessary nodes for pathing. Need a frontier-based planner that directly targets unknown/free boundaries (frontier cells) by pathing via global graph. Implement frontier detection on belief map, choose target frontier (random or farthest), navigate via BFS, allow revisiting with tabu short-term memory to break cycles. Also handle case when no frontiers remain (exploration complete) or no path to target. Submit eval to measure against baseline 0.5502.
